name: l7_opening_hole_cap_12
description: At level 7, enforce a strict opening policy for the first 12 pieces that rejects placements increasing holes unless they immediately clear a line.
llm_notes: Failure episodes collapse almost immediately (avg_pieces=1.0) with very high holes (avg_holes=90.64), while successful episodes sustain play with lower holes (76.94). A first-12-piece hole cap targets the early-collapse regime before score optimization matters.

name: l7_bumpiness_guardrail
description: On level 7, prioritize placements that keep local surface transitions smooth; avoid moves that sharply increase bumpiness unless they clear lines.
llm_notes: Bumpiness separates outcomes strongly (failure 3.09 vs success 1.38, delta 1.71). Height is similarly terminal in both groups (~19), so controlling surface volatility is a better discriminator than pure height minimization.

name: tetra_survive_tall_boards_hole_cap_opening_gate
description: Keep the tall-board survival + hole-cap logic, but activate a stricter variant during the opening phase (first 12 pieces) to prevent immediate terminal trajectories.
llm_notes: Parent focuses on height>75% states, but current failures die at avg_pieces=1.0, indicating the key decision window is earlier. Novel L7 outliers (164-243 lines, surprise up to 5.75) imply viable trajectories exist if the opening avoids catastrophic early board damage.
